name: Auster
description: Auster, also known as Notus in Greek mythology, is the Roman god of the south wind. He is typically depicted as an old man with a heavy cloak, often bringing with him the oppressive heat and moisture of the sirocco wind. Auster's influence extends to agriculture, as he is associated with the ripening of crops and the bringing of rain. However, his winds can also be destructive, capable of causing storms and damage to harvests. He is seen as a powerful and sometimes unpredictable force of nature, both beneficial and detrimental to human endeavors. Roman poets frequently invoked Auster to describe the changing seasons and the impact of weather on the landscape. He is often linked to the Mediterranean Sea, where his winds are particularly influential. While not as prominent as other Roman deities like Jupiter or Mars, Auster played a significant role in the daily lives of farmers and sailors, who depended on the winds for their livelihoods and safety. His presence served as a constant reminder of the power of nature and the importance of respecting its forces. Auster's character is complex, reflecting the ambivalent relationship between humans and the environment. He embodies both the life-giving and destructive potential of the natural world, making him a compelling figure in Roman mythology.
subtitle: The Roman God of the South Wind

keyMyths.title: Auster and the Harvest
keyMyths.description: Auster's winds can either help ripen crops or destroy them with storms. Farmers prayed to him for favorable winds to ensure a bountiful harvest.

keyMyths.title: Auster and the Sailors
keyMyths.description: Sailors relied on Auster's winds for navigation, but feared his sudden storms. They offered sacrifices to appease him for safe passage.
keyMyths.source: Roman sea shanties and maritime lore

keyMyths.title: Auster's Cloak
keyMyths.description: Auster is often depicted wearing a heavy cloak, representing the humidity and moisture he brings. This imagery is common in Roman art.

sources.author: Virgil
sources.description: Mentions Auster's role in storms and sea travel.
sources.text: Mentions Auster's role in storms and sea travel.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: References Auster's influence on the weather and seasons.
sources.text: References Auster's influence on the weather and seasons.

sources.author: Pliny the Elder
sources.description: Describes Auster's impact on agriculture and climate.
sources.text: Describes Auster's impact on agriculture and climate.
